title: fumaria capreolata var. bastardii
intro: Fumaria capreolata var. bastardii is a lesser-known variety of the Fumaria capreolata plant, also known as the caprifig or shepherd's cress, which belongs to the fumitory family and is a perennial herb native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Fumaria capreolata var. bastardii are said to include relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, as well as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tincture or tea, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
